contents.value: **.. note::** — reStructuredText keyword.
**.. warning::** — reStructuredText keyword.
**.. tip::** — reStructuredText keyword.
**.. important::** — reStructuredText keyword.
**.. danger::** — reStructuredText keyword.
**:ref:** — Built-in function/command in reStructuredText.
**:doc:** — Built-in function/command in reStructuredText.
**:func:** — Built-in function/command in reStructuredText.
**:class:** — Built-in function/command in reStructuredText.
**:meth:** — Built-in function/command in reStructuredText.

A **directive** is a command that controls document structure or behavior.
```rst
.. note::
   This is a note.
```

A **role** applies inline formatting or semantics.
```rst
:emphasis:`text`  :code:`code`  :ref:`label`
```
A **section** is created by underlining a title with `=`, `-`, `~`, etc.

A **reference** links to another location.
```rst
`Link text <URL>`_   :ref:`label`   :doc:`document`
```

A **substitution** defines reusable content.
```rst
.. |name| replace:: replacement text
```

A **literal block** preserves formatting for code.
```rst
::

   code here
```
**Lists** use `*`, `-`, `1.` for unordered/ordered items.

**Tables** can use grid or simple format.
```rst
+------+------+
| Col1 | Col2 |
+------+------+
```

A **footnote** adds supplementary information.
```rst
Text [1]_

.. [1] Footnote text.
```

The **image** directive embeds graphics.
```rst
.. image:: path/to/image.png
   :alt: Description
```

**toctree** creates a table of contents linking to other documents.
```rst
.. toctree::
   page1
   page2
```

An **admonition** highlights important information.
```rst
.. warning:: Be careful!
.. tip:: Helpful hint.
```

A **field list** provides metadata.
```rst
:author: Name
:version: 1.0
```

A **docstring** documents a Python object.
```rst
:param name: Description
:returns: Description
:raises Error: When condition.
```

A **domain** provides specialized directives for a language.
```rst
.. py:function:: func(arg)
.. js:class:: ClassName
```

```restructuredtext
.. code-block:: language
   :linenos:
   :emphasize-lines: 1,3

   code content
```
Displays a block of source code with syntax highlighting for the specified language, supporting line numbers, emphasized lines, caption text, and dedent options for documentation.

```restructuredtext
.. figure:: path/to/image.png
   :alt: Alt text
   :figwidth: 80%
   :align: center

   Caption text
```
Inserts an image wrapped in a figure element with an optional caption and legend, providing semantic grouping for illustrations with descriptive text below them.

```restructuredtext
.. note::

   Note content paragraph
```
Creates a visually distinct admonition block to highlight important supplementary information that readers should pay attention to but is not critical or dangerous.

```restructuredtext
.. warning::

   Warning content paragraph
```
Creates a prominent admonition block to alert readers about potentially dangerous operations, breaking changes, or situations that could cause data loss or errors.

```restructuredtext
:ref:`label-name` or :ref:`Display Text <label-name>`
```
Creates a cross-reference link to a labeled location anywhere in the documentation set, automatically resolving the section title or using custom display text.

```restructuredtext
:doc:`document-path` or :doc:`Display Text <document-path>`
```
Creates a hyperlink to another document in the documentation tree using the document name without file extension, with optional custom display text override.

```restructuredtext
.. automodule:: module_name
   :members:
   :undoc-members:
   :show-inheritance:
```
Sphinx autodoc directive that automatically extracts and formats documentation from Python module docstrings, class definitions, and function signatures into structured output.

```restructuredtext
.. literalinclude:: path/to/file.py
   :language: python
   :lines: 1-10
   :emphasize-lines: 3,5
```
Includes the contents of an external file as a literal code block with syntax highlighting, supporting line range selection and emphasis for documentation examples.

```restructuredtext
.. glossary::
   :sorted:

   term
      Definition of the term.
```
Defines a glossary of terms that can be referenced elsewhere using the :term: role, automatically generating definition list formatting with optional alphabetical sorting.

```restructuredtext
.. deprecated:: version
   Description of deprecation and migration path.
```
Marks a feature or API as deprecated since the specified version, providing a visible notice with migration guidance for users of the documented software.

```restructuredtext
String
```
Text data type representing a sequence of characters.

```restructuredtext
Number
```
Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

```restructuredtext
Boolean
```
Logical type with two values: true and false.

```restructuredtext
Array
```
Ordered collection of elements accessible by index.

```restructuredtext
Object
```
Collection of key-value pairs (properties and methods).

```restructuredtext
null
```
Represents the intentional absence of any value.

```restructuredtext
undefined
```
Represents an uninitialized or missing value.

```restructuredtext
print(value)
```
Output a value to the standard output stream.

```restructuredtext
length
```
Returns the number of elements in a collection or characters in a string.
